item.title: ByteDance’s new AI video generation model, Dreamina Seedance 2.0, comes to CapCut
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The new model in CapCut will have built-in protections for making video from real faces or unauthorized intellectual property.

item.title: Why hiring the weirdos works
item.dc:creator: Maggie Nye, Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: When you’re building at breakneck speed, hiring a trusted team is crucial for an early-stage startup. Bland CEO and co-founder Isaiah Granet has tactical advice on how the company managed to find hidden talent in unlikely places.

item.title: A major hacking tool has leaked online, putting millions of iPhones at risk. Here’s what you need to know.
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Here’s what we know, and what you need to know, about Coruna and DarkSword, two advanced iPhone hacking tools discovered by security researchers. DarkSword has now leaked online.

item.title: Conntour raises $7M from General Catalyst, YC to build an AI search engine for security video systems
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: Conntour uses AI models to let security teams query camera feeds using natural language to find any object, person, or situation.

item.title: Cohere launches an open-source voice model specifically for transcription
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Relatively light at just 2 billion parameters, the model is meant for use with consumer-grade GPUs for those who want to self-host it. It currently supports 14 languages.

item.title: WhatsApp can now draft AI-generated responses based on your conversations
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The Meta-owned messaging platform is also launching a way to free up space, the ability to touch up photos with Meta AI, and more.

item.title: A ‘pound of flesh’ from data centers: one senator’s answer to AI job losses
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Fears of AI-driven job loss are growing fast, and they’re fueling backlash against data centers. Sen. Mark Warner suggests taxing them to help workers survive the transition.

item.title: Mistral releases a new open-source model for speech generation
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Mistral's new speech model can run on a smartwatch or a smartphone.

item.title: A little-known Croatian startup is coming for the robotaxi market with help from Uber
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Verne, the robotaxi company launched under Rimac Group, is starting with a service in Zagreb, Croatia.

item.title: The least surprising chapter of the Manus story is what’s happening right now
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Did anyone think there would not be a reckoning over this tie-up?

item.title: Mercor competitor Deccan AI raises $25M, sources experts from India
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Deccan AI concentrates its workforce in India to manage quality in a fast-growing but fragmented AI training market.

item.title: Silicon Valley’s two biggest dramas have intersected: LiteLLM and Delve
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: LiteLLM offers an AI open source project used by millions that was infected by credential harvesting malware.

item.title: The AI skills gap is here, says AI company, and power users are pulling ahead
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic finds AI isn’t replacing jobs yet, but early data shows growing inequality as experienced users gain an edge, raising concerns about future displacement and workforce divides.

item.title: Convicted spyware chief hints that Greece’s government was behind dozens of phone hacks
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The spyware founder's comments are the most direct suggestion yet from anyone inside Intellexa that the Mitsotakis government authorized the hacking of dozens of phones belonging to senior Greek government ministers, opposition leaders, military officials, and journalists.

item.title: Who’s driving Waymo’s self-driving cars? Sometimes, the police.
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: First responders have had to take control of Waymo vehicles and move them during emergency situations, including in at least two active crime scenes, TechCrunch found.

item.title: Google unveils TurboQuant, a new AI memory compression algorithm — and yes, the internet is calling it ‘Pied Piper’
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google’s TurboQuant has the internet joking about Pied Piper from HBO's "Silicon Valley." The compression algorithm promises to shrink AI’s “working memory” by up to 6x, but it’s still just a lab experiment for now.

item.title: Melania Trump wants a robot to homeschool your child
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The first lady sees AI and robotics playing a prominent role in the future of American education.

item.title: Riding the GLP-1 boom, VITL lands $7.5M to overhaul cash-pay clinic prescribing
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The startup provides an e-prescribing marketplace for the booming cash-pay clinic market.

item.title: Jury finds Meta and Google negligent in landmark social media addiction trial
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Evidence presented at the trial swayed the jury to the plaintiff's side, demonstrating that Meta understood how addictive its platforms could be among teens in particular and that it was actively researching the issue and using its findings to increase engagement among young users.

item.title: Meta is cutting several hundred jobs
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Meta is laying off several hundred employees across multiple teams, including sales, recruiting, and the Reality Labs division. The cuts will impact employees in the U.S. and other international markets.
